name: Canning (Dry) Beans with Tomato or Molasses Sauce
recipeIngredient: An average of 5 pounds of beans is needed per canner load of 7 quarts
An average of 3-1/4 pounds is needed per canner load of 9 pints
An average of 3/4 pound per quart
recipeInstructions:
1. Select mature, dry seeds and sort out and discard discolored seeds.
2. Sort and wash dry beans.
3. Add 3 cups of water for each cup of dried beans or peas.
4. Boil 2 minutes, remove from heat, soak for 1 hour, and drain.
5. Heat to boiling in fresh water and save the liquid for making sauce.
6. Make your choice of sauces:
- Tomato Sauce: Mix 1 quart tomato juice, 3 tablespoons sugar, 2 teaspoons salt, 1 tablespoon chopped onion, and 1/4 teaspoon each of ground cloves, allspice, mace, and cayenne pepper. Alternatively, mix 1 cup tomato ketchup with 3 cups cooking liquid from beans.
- Molasses Sauce: Mix 4 cups water or cooking liquid from beans, 3 tablespoons dark molasses, 1 tablespoon vinegar, 2 teaspoons salt, and 3/4 teaspoon powdered dry mustard.
7. Fill jars three-fourths full with hot beans.
8. Add a 3/4-inch cube of pork, ham, or bacon to each jar if desired.
9. Fill jars with heated sauce, leaving 1-inch headspace.
10. Adjust lids and process according to the recommendations in Table 1 or Table 2 based on the canning method used.
Table 1: Recommended processing time for Beans, Dry, with Tomato or Molasses Sauce in a dial-gauge pressure canner
- Style of Pack: Hot
- Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
- Process Time: 65 minutes for Pints, 75 minutes for Quarts
- Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 2,000 ft: 11 lb
Table 2: Recommended processing time for Beans, Dry, with Tomato or Molasses Sauce in a weighted-gauge pressure canner
- Style of Pack: Hot
- Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
- Process Time: 65 minutes for Pints, 75 minutes for Quarts
- Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 lb
- Above 1,000 ft: 15 lb
